core_mechanism: stability analysis of the equilibria of a system of coupled nonlinear rate equations (Lotka-Volterra-type interacting-population dynamics) on a network of mutually interacting units, determining when such a network admits a stable steady state as a function of its interaction topology, together with an iterative node-addition process that grows the network toward large stable configurations
brief_reason: The abstract's stated focus is 'the stability of such webs' modelled as the population dynamics of several interacting species (a coupled dynamical system on a network), while the assembly/evolutionary variants add units via 'invasion' or 'speciation' to iteratively build large stable networks.
